region_of_origin: USA — KIND LLC; available at Cold Storage and Takashimaya Food Hall, Singapore
food_subgroup: Whole nut and coconut snack bar; 40g bar; fat-dominant (35g fat per 100g); GI ~52; coconut-derived saturated fat prominent

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for a branded Kind Bar nut-and-coconut snack bar. Closest parent concept is a generic nut bar or cereal/nut snack bar. URI left null to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cereal and nut bars — closest defensible FoodEx2 category for a whole-nut snack bar bound with honey/glucose syrup
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJC (cereal and nut bars) is the closest defensible parent. No exact code exists for the Kind Bar Almond & Coconut branded product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded nut-and-coconut snack bar. Left null to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: bound with honey and glucose syrup

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Kind Bar Almond & Coconut serving is one bar weighing 40g, providing approximately 200 kcal, 14g fat, 5g protein, 16g carbohydrate, and 9g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: KIND Bar, Almond & Coconut — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) + KIND LLC Singapore product label
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for KIND Bar Almond & Coconut, cross-referenced against the KIND LLC Singapore market product label available at Cold Storage and Takashimaya Food Hall. Per-100g values are derived by scaling the 40g bar label values. Values are consistent between USDA Branded entry and Singapore label.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of nut-containing snack bars — analogous estimate based on almond-and-honey bound nut bars; University of Sydney GI Database reference for mixed nut bars
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the KIND Bar Almond & Coconut in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~52 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for whole-almond nut bars bound with honey/glucose syrup (GI range 50–55), consistent with the fat-slowing mechanism of high-fat nut bars. GL per 40g serving = (52 × 16g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 8.3. Confidence 0.9. Source: University of Sydney GI Database closest analogous entries for nut bars.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (15mg per 40g bar) is beneficial; however, 7g saturated fat per bar from coconut raises LDL-C and cardiovascular risk in hypertensive patients with metabolic syndrome
hypertension.guidance: Kind Bar Almond & Coconut is sodium-safe for hypertension (15mg per bar), but daily consumption should be monitored in hypertensive patients with metabolic syndrome due to 7g saturated fat per bar from coconut, which may contribute to LDL-C elevation and compound cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~52 and GL ~8.3 per 40g bar are moderate-low; 9g sugar per bar requires portion awareness in T2DM patients
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 52 and a glycaemic load of 8.3 per bar, Kind Bar Almond & Coconut is an acceptable occasional snack for T2DM patients, but the 9g sugar per bar warrants portion control and should not be consumed alongside other high-GI foods in the same meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: 7g saturated fat per 40g bar (17.5g per 100g) from coconut represents 35% of the daily saturated fat limit; partially offset by almond MUFA and Vitamin E
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Cardiovascular patients should limit Kind Bar Almond & Coconut to occasional consumption given that one 40g bar delivers 7g saturated fat (35% of the 20g daily limit), predominantly from coconut; the almond-derived MUFA and Vitamin E (5mg per bar, 33% RDA) provide partial cardiovascular benefit but do not offset the saturated fat burden at daily intake levels.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High Vitamin E (5mg per 40g bar = 33% RDA) and magnesium are anti-inflammatory; 7g saturated fat from coconut per bar is a concern in an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: For rheumatoid arthritis patients, the Vitamin E and magnesium from almonds offer anti-inflammatory benefit, but the 7g saturated fat per bar from coconut should be monitored to avoid undermining an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kind Bar Almond & Coconut (40g) is a whole-nut snack bar manufactured by KIND LLC (USA) and available in Singapore at Cold Storage supermarkets and Takashimaya Food Hall. The bar is cold-pressed and not baked, binding whole almonds and shredded coconut with honey and glucose syrup to form a chewy, dense bar with visible whole nuts. It is fat-dominant: 35g fat per 100g (14g per 40g bar), of which 17.5g per 100g (7g per bar) is saturated fat derived primarily from coconut. Protein is moderate at 12.5g per 100g (5g per bar). Carbohydrate is 40g per 100g (16g per bar) with 22.5g sugar per 100g (9g per bar). Energy is 500 kcal per 100g (200 kcal per bar). Dietary fibre is 5g per 100g (2g per bar), partly from chicory root fibre. Sodium is very low at 37.5mg per 100g (15mg per bar), making it sodium-safe for hypertension. The glycaemic index is approximately 52 (low-medium) with a glycaemic load of 8.3 per bar, as the high fat content slows glucose absorption from the honey and glucose binder. Key micronutrients include Vitamin E (5mg per 100g; 5mg per bar = 33% RDA), magnesium (60mg per 100g; 24mg per bar), potassium (250mg per 100g), and calcium (75mg per 100g). Cholesterol is 0mg. Trans fat is 0g. The bar is vegetarian, halal-certified, gluten-free, and dairy-free. For Type 2 Diabetes: acceptable as an occasional snack given moderate GL (~8.3), but 9g sugar per bar requires portion awareness. For hypertension: excellent sodium profile but saturated fat accumulation with daily use requires monitoring. For cardiovascular disease: the 7g saturated fat per bar (35% daily limit) from coconut is the primary concern; almond MUFA and Vitamin E provide partial offset. The visible whole-nut format creates a perception of naturalness that is partially justified by the almond micronutrient profile but should not lead to over-consumption.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for KIND Bar Almond & Coconut, cross-referenced with the KIND LLC Singapore market product label. Per-100g values are derived by scaling the verified 40g bar label values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable whole-almond nut bars (confidence 0.9). All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown (MUFA/PUFA split) is not separately reported on the KIND Bar Singapore product label or in the USDA FoodData Central Branded entry for this specific SKU as of April 2026; total fat and saturated fat are confirmed. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported on product label or in USDA Branded entry for this SKU. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per product label. cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 per product label. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are confirmed 0.0 per product label (not present in this formulation). GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for whole-almond honey-bound nut bars (GI range 50–55); no direct GI measurement for this specific KIND Bar SKU was found in the database. GL per serving = (52 × 16g available carbohydrate per 40g bar) / 100 = 8.3. FoodEx2 code A0FJC (cereal and nut bars) is the closest defensible parent category; no exact branded code exists. FoodOn URI left null as no exact FoodOn concept exists for this branded product. SNOMED CT code left null as no applicable clinical food concept exists for this branded snack bar.
